qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated users can remotely restart the Ajenti master service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets `POST /api/core/restart-master` and a forced master-service restart; the advisory concerns non-superuser custom package installation in the `auth_users` task/plugin-install path, not the restart endpoint.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described custom-package installation root cause. The only final qualifying finding is a different missing-authorization issue on the restart-master endpoint.
evidence: The advisory summary/details describe an authenticated non-superuser using the `auth_users` plugin to install a custom package.
The only final qualifying finding report exercises `POST /api/core/restart-master` and documents availability impact from restarting the Ajenti master service.

Upstream Ajenti source/history for the advisory area points to the task and plugin-install path (`/api/core/tasks/start` and `aj.plugins.plugins.tasks.InstallPlugin`), which is distinct from the reported restart-master control path.
